§6Spent Fuel Pool Drum§f

§aCool down your hot depleted fuel before recycling — essential for fuel reprocessing!§f

The §bSpent Fuel Pool Drum§f is a container that holds §e12§f hot depleted nuclear fuel rods and cools them down for recycling. Hot fuel is pyrophoric and highly radioactive — it must be cooled before any further processing.

§6Crafting§f:
§b• 4×§f Steel Ingot
§b• 4×§f Iron Bars
§b• 3×§f Empty Quad Rod

§6Usage§f:
§b• Place underwater§f — surround the block with water
§b• Insert hot depleted fuel§f into the drum's GUI
§b• Wait for cooling§f — more water contact = faster cooling
§b• Average time§f: §e5 minutes§f, but deviation ranges from §e2 seconds§f to §e1 hour§f

§6Cooled Fuel Processing§f: Once cooled, the fuel can be processed in a §bPUREX§f or §bCentrifuge§f to extract useful isotopes like §bTechnetium-99§f, §bUranium-238§f, and §bPlutonium§f nuggets.

§c⚠ Safety§f: Depleted fuel and fuel rods are highly radioactive and pyrophoric. Use a §bTungsten Reacher§f and hazmat suit! Setup an automated conveyor chain if possible.
